reviews.text:
Mark Rees agreed to represent our case. We paid the retainer fee & agreed to pay over 10,000 just to get a call 3 days (!!!) before court saying there was a conflict of interest and he could no longer represent us. leaving us with 3 days to find another lawyer and expect them to get up to date on an emergency case. Extremely unprofessional and inconsiderate.

according to his reply, he immediately notified us about the conflict as soon as they figured it out. in fact, he notified other lawyers (those he recommended) about the issue the 18th, but did not notify us until the 19th after 10am. the other lawyer he recommended is the one who told us he’d been awaiting our call the entire night but, again, we weren’t notified until almost noon the next day. whenever i stated that to them in person, saying we would have appreciated being told immediately so we had a better chance to be prepared, they apologized saying “it just got so busy up here”.

And, we retained services the 8th. Before vacation. I’ll attach paperwork to prove it. Thank you for reading, please be cautious when retaining services.
reviews.ownerResponse:
We appreciate you taking the time to share your feedback, Jade, and we're truly sorry for the frustration and inconvenience this situation caused. Our firm strives to provide the highest level of ethical and professional service to every client, and we regret that we were unable to proceed with your case due to an unforeseen conflict of interest.

To clarify, Your husband retained our services on August 11, 2025, while I was on a pre-scheduled vacation (August 9-16, 2025), which was discussed upfront. Upon my return to the office on August 18, 2025, we received notification from the opposing party regarding a prior consultation with an involved individual over two years ago. After promptly verifying this, we immediately informed your husband of the conflict to ensure compliance with ethical standards, recommended alternative attorneys experienced in similar matters, and issued a full refund of the $7,500 retainer that same day.

We understand the timing was challenging with the emergency hearing scheduled for August 22, 2025, and we apologize for any added stress this created. If there's anything further we can do to assist, such as providing additional referrals or clarification, please don't hesitate to reach out directly.

Thank you for your understanding.

Mark Rees

The person on the phone said  "sign it,just go ahead and sign it" I asked "ARE you sure?" the guy said "Yes". reviews.text: Way to picky
reviews.ownerResponse: We searched our records, and we were unable to locate anyone named Donald Schultz in our system. Perhaps you have us confused with another law firm or go by a different name. In any event, whether you have contacted our office before or not, please know NST Law makes every effort to help everyone that we possibly can, but we must review the facts presented to us and operate within the bounds of the law when determining whether we can assist someone by taking their case. For the last 30+ years, our law firm’s main goal has been to help people injured through no fault of their own obtain justice and fair compensation for their injuries. There may be several reasons why we may not be able to help someone who calls our office. Some of the reasons may include that the caller seeking representation was at fault, there was no insurance, there were no injuries, or the time period (statute of limitations) for filing suit has expired. If we cannot help someone, we always try to personally explain our reasons for not being able to undertake representation. Please feel free to contact us in the future if you are in need of a personal injury attorney. We wish you the best going forward.

At the third appearance in August, the trial finally began. Ben had all the evidence needed to prove innocence, but it was clear he hadn’t reviewed it thoroughly. He was unprepared to argue the case (wasted money), and the judge declined to make a decision that day.

Since September 2024, we still have no verdict. Ben does not return phone calls, and while he occasionally replies to texts, his responses are vague and he never directly answers our questions.

reviews.text: As a young man entering the workforce, I was fortunate to be hired by a major corporation that offers a sound retirement plan. I saved because the option was made easy by the corporation. As time accumulated I began to understand the principle and need to save for my latter years. Due to a change in corporate ownership, I was given the opportunity to manage my existing retirement fund while continuing to build a new 401K with the new corporation. I considered how I should invest my retirement funds and soon came to the conclusion that I was not qualified to manage my retirement funds. I tried several funds managers and found one, a major funds management company, that did a great job of protecting and growing my retirement portfolio. Prior to retiring, the business I worked for changed hands twice. I rolled each 401K into my managed account and continued to see success in my retirement funds. However a few years into retirement I began to seek a more holistic approach. My existing funds management company was very good at growing my retirement funds. However they lacked the resources and expertise to help me plan my life in retirement and legacy in my golden years. I found a funds management team that listened, understood my vision and had the expertise to achieve my goals in Quraishi Law & Wealth. With their SMART Wealth Planning tools they were able to create a customized plan to meet my goal of growing my wealth, lowering my taxes and creating a lasting legacy. My one regret is that I did not discover Quraishi Law & Wealth earlier in my financial travels/growth. They are a great firm that offered me a much needed personal and holistic approach to what can be a frustrating endeavor with some financial planning organizations and/or financial managers.

Disappointing Legal Representation by Attorney Bryce Cook

I am deeply disappointed with the legal services provided by Attorney Bryce Cook. Despite my nephew being a dedicated father and diligently fulfilling all requests made by Attorney Cook in a timely manner, the outcome of the case was far from satisfactory.

Despite the sensitive nature of the case, my family decided to invest in professional legal representation, trusting Attorney Cook to navigate us through the legal complexities. However, Attorney Cook failed to meet our expectations on multiple fronts.

Firstly, Attorney Cook failed to ensure that the other party was properly served, a basic and essential step in legal proceedings. This negligence significantly hindered the progress of the case and undermined our confidence in Attorney Cook's competence.

Furthermore, despite assurances, Attorney Cook failed to provide a comprehensive plan of action, leaving us feeling adrift and uninformed about the direction of the case. This lack of communication and transparency only exacerbated our concerns and frustrations.

To add insult to injury, Attorney Cook ceased all communication regarding the payment made for his services and ignored requests for a refund. This unprofessional behavior demonstrates a blatant disregard for ethical and professional standards.

In conclusion, based on my firsthand experience, I cannot in good conscience recommend Attorney Bryce Cook to anyone seeking legal representation.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Shirley,

Thank you for your feedback. Unfortunately each case, each client, and each situation is unique with its own complexities. Holidays thrown in compounds delays in working out court dates. Some types of filings require more client provided information before filing as well. That is somewhat beyond our control.

We show many calls you made were during the time we publicly post that the office is closed, for example, during lunch. I do not expect staff to answer phones, email or texts during their posted lunch, after hours, or when on leave or holiday. I'm sorry if that did not meet your expectation. Regardless of whether you call once or ten times during lunch, for example, there will be no answer, but you will get a response at our earliest opportunity, as we work around court settings, messages, and appointments, or we will answer during available times if you call then.

I've never known staff to be rude, I do know there can be misunderstandings. We will look into that. I have generally observed that sometimes what one person intends as friendly, playful, or direct may be offensive to someone else. If we catch something like that happening we address the misunderstanding. But this is the first we are hearing of it. Thank you for bringing it to our attention. We will be more vigilant.

Each case requires a service agreement. We do occasionally decline to request additional retainer funds or fees, even when the retainer is depleated and additional fees are earned and justified, but we do so on a case by case basis. If we do give a break in one situation, it does not mean it will happen in other situations, even with the same client. If you have specific questions about billing, please let me know.

Best of luck to you. I hope your new arrangement works out for you and better serves your current needs. Merry Christmas. May God bless you and your family.
